message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 2018","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13277","justification":"National survey for Germany including detailed modules on work, employment conditions, and digital aspects of working life; 2018 fieldwork lies within the 2010s and allows analysis of digitalization and telecommuting in relation to work behaviour and performance proxies."},{"title":"German General Social Survey (ALLBUS) 2014","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12119","justification":"Large-scale German survey from the mid‑2010s with questions on employment, working conditions, and social change; suitable as a 2010s benchmark for examining digitalization and telework arrangements alongside job attitudes and self‑reported performance indicators."},{"title":"Panel Study Labour Market and Social Security (PASS), Version 15.0 (Waves 2006–2021)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/pw-pass-15.0.0","justification":"German longitudinal survey that covers labour market participation, employment forms, and job quality; the 2010s waves contain variables on flexible and home-based work arrangements and allow linking telecommuting and digitalization to employment intensity and performance-related outcomes."},{"title":"BIBB/BAuA Employment Survey of the Working Population on Qualification and Working Conditions in Germany 2012","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.7803/500.12.1.1.10","justification":"Representative German survey focused on working conditions and job requirements; includes information on ICT use, digital tools, and flexible work organisation in 2012, enabling analysis of digitalization, telecommuting opportunities, and their relation to workload and performance‑related indicators."},{"title":"BIBB/BAuA Employment Survey of the Working Population on Qualification and Working Conditions in Germany 2018","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.7803/501.18.1.1.10","justification":"Updated German employment survey in the late 2010s with detailed questions on digital technologies at work, mobile/tele work, workloads and strain; well suited for studying links between digitalization, telecommuting, long hours (workaholism proxies) and perceived job performance."},{"title":"Familienfreundlichkeit in deutschen Unternehmen 2015 (Family-Friendliness of German Companies, 2015)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12286","justification":"Firm-level survey in Germany including items on flexible and family-oriented work arrangements such as home office and telecommuting; 2015 timing allows study of organisational digitalization practices and how they relate to expectations about employees’ availability and performance."},{"title":"German Socio-Economic Panel (SOEP), GESIS Edition (longitudinal, incl. 2010s waves)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.5684/soep-core.v38eu","justification":"Large, long-running panel for Germany with rich employment and work-time information; 2010s waves contain data on working from home, overtime, and work intensity enabling construction of workaholism-like measures and their association with job outcomes."},{"title":"BIBB/BAuA Employment Survey 2012 – Scientific Use File at GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA5966","justification":"Direct GESIS landing page for the 2012 BIBB/BAuA Employment Survey; provides variable documentation for ICT use, telework and strain, which can be used to operationalise digitalization, telecommuting, and workaholic tendencies vis‑à‑vis performance proxies."},{"title":"BIBB/BAuA Employment Survey 2018 – Scientific Use File at GESIS","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA7571","justification":"GESIS landing page for the 2018 employment survey in Germany; metadata show coverage of digitised work processes, mobile work and workload, directly relevant to studying digitalization, telecommuting, long working hours and job performance perceptions in the late 2010s."},{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2015, GESIS Edition (Germany subsample)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12560","justification":"Cross‑national survey including a German sample; questionnaire covers ICT use, working from home, work intensity, and self‑reported job performance. The 2015 wave lies in the 2010s and can be restricted to Germany for analysis of digitalization, telecommuting, and workaholic work patterns."}]}
